pi_items.source: Examined /workspace/data/patients.sql containing all 20 pre-loaded Synthea patients. Performed grep search for 'Maria' and 'maria' with no matches found.
Examined /workspace/data/patients.sql containing all 20 pre-loaded Synthea patients. Performed grep search for 'Rodriguez' and 'rodriguez' with no matches found.
Examined /workspace/data/patients.sql. Performed grep search for '1978-06-14' with no matches found. 20 pre-loaded patients have DOBs ranging from 1954-02-19 to 2006-12-04.

Task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) confirm this path is used for the output file. The path is correctly set in export_result.sh line 7: OUTPUT_PATH="/home/ga/Documents/maria_rodriguez_ccda.xml"

Web search indicates typical CCDA XML files range from hundreds of kilobytes to several megabytes. 1000 bytes is a very minimal CCDA, but technically possible for a sparse document. Cannot verify without actual execution.

privileged_info_summary: The task claims to export a C-CDA document for patient Maria Rodriguez (DOB: 1978-06-14). However, this patient does NOT exist in the pre-loaded Synthea synthetic patient dataset. The environment loads exactly 20 pre-loaded patients from /workspace/data/patients.sql, and Maria Rodriguez is not among them. The complete list of pre-loaded patients is: Conchita Hernandes, Corine Ziemann, Crysta Parisian, Charles Nolan, Kent Zemlak, Dwight Dach, Ezequiel Hermiston, Denny Lubowitz, Kelle Crist, Sherill Botsford, Hobert Wuckert, Malka Hartmann, Cordie King, Coreen Treutel, Tracey Crona, Myrtis Armstrong, Arlie McClure, Crystal Schroeder, Luann Sanford, and Horacio Santacruz. The task uses NOSH ChartingSystem, an open-source EHR that supports C-CDA exports per official documentation. The output path /home/ga/Documents/maria_rodriguez_ccda.xml is correctly specified in the task scripts.
